task_completion.visual_evidence: The Avare application's main screen or map view is visible.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Preferences or Settings menu
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Avare Preferences or Settings menu is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Locate the CAP Grid overlay setting
task_completion.visual_evidence: A setting labeled 'CAP Grids', 'Civil Air Patrol', or similar is visible in the preferences list.

task_completion.description: Enable the CAP Grid overlay setting
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox or toggle switch next to the CAP Grids setting is checked or in the 'on' position.

task_completion.description: Return to the main map view
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application has returned to the primary navigational map view.

task_completion.description: Ensure CAP grid lines are visible on the map
task_completion.visual_evidence: Distinct grid lines (representing the Civil Air Patrol grids) are visibly overlaid on top of the map.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to change settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show navigation through Avare's menus rather than terminal commands or ADB shell executions.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing the preferences file
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editor or file manager is shown modifying the com.ds.avare_preferences.xml file.

integrity.description: The agent verified the grid on the actual Avare map
integrity.visual_evidence: The map view showing the grid lines is clearly within the genuine Avare app interface, not a static image, web browser search, or external application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Avare preferences file is located at /data/data/com.ds.avare/shared_prefs/com.ds.avare_preferences.xml. When the CAP Grid overlay is enabled via the GUI, the boolean value 'true' is written to the 'CAPGrids' key.
